families.name: Debian
families.description: One of the oldest and most influential distributions, a community-driven universal OS.

families.name: Ubuntu
families.description: Debian derivative focused on ease-of-use and a regular six-month release cadence.

families.name: Linux Mint
families.description: Ubuntu- and Debian-based distro designed for elegance and simplicity.

families.name: Kali
families.description: Debian-derived penetration-testing and security-auditing distribution.

families.name: Tails
families.description: Live, amnesic incognito system focused on privacy and anonymity.

families.name: Parrot
families.description: Debian-based security, pentesting, and forensics OS.

families.name: Devuan
families.description: systemd-free fork of Debian.

families.name: Trisquel
families.description: Ubuntu-based fully-free software distribution endorsed by the FSF.

families.name: PureOS
families.description: Privacy-focused, libre Debian-based distribution.

families.name: Arch
families.description: Rolling-release distribution centered on simplicity and the KISS principle.

families.name: Manjaro
families.description: User-friendly Arch derivative with its own tooling and repositories.

families.name: Endeavour
families.description: Arch-based rolling distro with a friendly installer and forum-driven ethos.

families.name: ArcoLinux
families.description: Arch-based distro focused on learning, with extensive choice of DEs.

families.name: Garuda
families.description: Arch-based performance-oriented Linux distro with a striking UI.

families.name: Artix
families.description: Arch-based, init-fork-friendly distro with OpenRC, Runit, and s6.

families.name: BlackArch
families.description: Arch-based penetration-testing distribution with thousands of security tools.

families.name: CachyOS
families.description: Arch-based distro with custom kernel and performance-oriented optimizations.

families.name: RHEL
families.description: Red Hat Enterprise Linux, the commercial-grade upstream for many server distros.

families.name: Fedora
families.description: Community-led RHEL upstream with the latest GNU/Linux innovations.

families.name: CentOS
families.description: Community rebuild of RHEL. CentOS Linux was discontinued; CentOS Stream continues.

families.name: Rocky
families.description: RHEL-compatible community distro founded by one of the original CentOS creators.

families.name: AlmaLinux
families.description: Community-driven RHEL fork focused on stability and binary compatibility.

families.name: Oracle Linux
families.description: RHEL-compatible, supported by Oracle with optional Unbreakable Enterprise Kernel.

families.name: Amazon Linux
families.description: AWS-optimized Linux for EC2 services, based on RHEL.

families.name: Mageia
families.description: Fork of Mandriva Linux maintained by former Mandriva contributors.

families.name: Mandriva
families.description: Originally MandrakeSoft, a Red Hat derivative popular in the early 2000s. Discontinued.

families.name: OpenMandriva
families.description: Community successor to Mandriva Linux.

families.name: Slackware
families.description: One of the oldest actively maintained distros; values simplicity and Unix philosophy.

families.name: Salix
families.description: Slackware-based, simplified and beginner-friendly.

families.name: Porteus
families.description: Slackware-based, tiny and fast-live-cd modular distro.

families.name: SliTaz
families.description: Slackware-inspired ultra-lightweight live distro.

families.name: Vector Linux
families.description: Slackware-based distro aimed at the casual user. Discontinued.

families.name: Absolute Linux
families.description: Lightweight Slackware-based distro aimed at newcomers. Discontinued.

families.name: Puppy
families.description: Tiny, easy-to-use Linux that runs entirely from RAM.

families.name: SUSE
families.description: German enterprise distribution dating back to 1994; upstream for openSUSE.

families.name: openSUSE
families.description: Community-driven SUSE derivative with Tumbleweed (rolling) and Leap (fixed).

families.name: Gentoo
families.description: Source-based, highly customizable distribution with Portage.

families.name: Calculate Linux
families.description: Gentoo-based Russian distro optimized for office and corporate use.

families.name: Funtoo
families.description: Gentoo-based, next-generation derivative by Daniel Robbins. Discontinued.

families.name: Sabayon
families.description: Gentoo-based, beginner-friendly binary distribution. Discontinued.

families.name: Pentoo
families.description: Gentoo-based security and penetration-testing distribution.

families.name: Alpine
families.description: Security-oriented, lightweight Linux using musl libc and BusyBox.

families.name: postmarketOS
families.description: Alpine-based distribution aimed at mobile phones and tablets.

families.name: Void
families.description: Independent, musl/libc-flexible distribution with its own runit-based init.

families.name: NixOS
families.description: Declarative, purely functional Nix-based distribution with atomic upgrades.

families.name: Solus
families.description: Independent, rolling distro with the Budgie desktop.

families.name: Raspberry Pi OS
families.description: Debian-based OS optimized for the Raspberry Pi family of single-board computers.

families.name: KNOPPIX
families.description: One of the first Live-CD Linux distributions, Debian-based.

families.name: antiX
families.description: Fast, lightweight Debian/MEPIS-based live distro for old hardware.

families.name: MX Linux
families.description: Mid-weight distro co-developed by antiX and MEPIS communities.

families.name: ChromeOS
families.description: Linux-kernel-based OS by Google centered on the Chrome browser.

families.name: OpenWrt
families.description: Linux for routers, gateways, and embedded networking devices.

families.name: Qubes
families.description: Security-by-compartmentalization OS built on Fedora/Xen.

distros.name: Linux kernel
distros.description: The kernel at the heart of every distro, originally released by Linus Torvalds on 25 August 1991.

distros.name: Debian
distros.description: Universal, community-built Debian. A founding influence for hundreds of derivatives worldwide.

distros.name: Ubuntu
distros.description: Debian derivative from Canonical with a six-monthly release cadence and long-term-support versions.

distros.name: Ubuntu Server
distros.description: Server-oriented Ubuntu release without a default desktop.

distros.name: Ubuntu Cloud
distros.description: Optimized cloud images for OpenStack and public clouds.

distros.name: Ubuntu Core
distros.description: Transactional, snap-based Ubuntu for IoT and embedded devices.

distros.name: Ubuntu Touch
distros.description: Mobile-platform Ubuntu for phones and tablets, maintained by UBports.

distros.name: Kubuntu
distros.description: Ubuntu with the KDE Plasma desktop.

distros.name: Xubuntu
distros.description: Lightweight Ubuntu with the Xfce desktop.

distros.name: Lubuntu
distros.description: Lightweight Ubuntu using LXQt, aimed at older hardware.

distros.name: Ubuntu MATE
distros.description: Ubuntu with the classic MATE desktop environment.

distros.name: Ubuntu Budgie
distros.description: Ubuntu with the elegant Budgie desktop.

distros.name: Ubuntu Kylin
distros.description: Ubuntu distribution variant tailored for Chinese users.

distros.name: Ubuntu Studio
distros.description: Ubuntu aimed at multimedia creation, audio, video, and graphics work.

distros.name: Edubuntu
distros.description: Educational Ubuntu tailored for classroom use.

distros.name: Ubuntu Cinnamon
distros.description: Ubuntu with the Cinnamon desktop, modeled after Linux Mint.

distros.name: Ubuntu Unity
distros.description: Community-maintained Ubuntu with the Unity desktop revived.

distros.name: Ubuntu GNOME
distros.description: Former Ubuntu flavour shipping the GNOME Shell as default. Merged back into Ubuntu.

distros.name: UbuntuDDE
distros.description: Ubuntu with the Deepin Desktop Environment, focused on a beautiful, friendly UI.

distros.name: Linux Mint
distros.description: Elegant, easy-to-use Ubuntu-derivative; one of the most popular desktop distros.

distros.name: Linux Mint Cinnamon
distros.description: Default Linux Mint edition with Cinnamon desktop.

distros.name: Linux Mint MATE
distros.description: Linux Mint edition with the MATE desktop.

distros.name: Linux Mint XFCE
distros.description: Linux Mint edition with XFCE for older hardware.

distros.name: LMDE
distros.description: Linux Mint Debian Edition: pure Debian base with the Mint desktop polish.

distros.name: elementary OS
distros.description: Polished, macOS-inspired Ubuntu derivative with Pantheon desktop.

distros.name: Pop!_OS
distros.description: System76's Ubuntu derivative for creators and developers with tiling-window ergonomics.

distros.name: Zorin OS
distros.description: Windows-like Ubuntu derivative aimed at newcomers migrating from Windows.

distros.name: KDE neon
distros.description: KDE Project's official reference distribution offering the very latest Plasma on Ubuntu LTS.

distros.name: Peppermint OS
distros.description: Cloud-focused lightweight Ubuntu derivative.

distros.name: Bodhi Linux
distros.description: Lightweight Ubuntu LTS-based distro with the Moksha desktop.

distros.name: CrunchBang++
distros.description: Community continuation of the original CrunchBang minimalistic Openbox Ubuntu.

distros.name: wattOS
distros.description: Lightweight Ubuntu derivative. Discontinued.

distros.name: Voyager Linux
distros.description: Ubuntu-based live distro with a striking, minimalist Xfce desktop.

distros.name: MakuluLinux
distros.description: Ubuntu-based, flashy window-manager distro. Discontinued.

distros.name: Pinguy OS
distros.description: Ubuntu-based distro aimed at ease of use. Discontinued.

distros.name: SnowLinux
distros.description: Debian-based, lightweight and elegant Linux distro. Discontinued.

distros.name: Septor
distros.description: Debian-based, privacy- and anonymity-focused live distro. Discontinued.

distros.name: BackBox Linux
distros.description: Ubuntu-based penetration-testing and security-assessment distro.

distros.name: BlackBuntu
distros.description: Ubuntu-based penetration-testing distro. Discontinued.

distros.name: Deepin
distros.description: Polished Debian-based Chinese distribution with its own DDE desktop.

distros.name: Kali Linux
distros.description: Debian-derived, security testing and forensics distribution maintained by Offensive Security.

distros.name: Kali NetHunter
distros.description: Mobile penetration-testing platform on Android.

distros.name: Parrot OS
distros.description: Debian-based, security, pentesting, and forensics distribution.

distros.name: Parrot Home
distros.description: Daily-driver edition of Parrot OS without the pentesting tools.

distros.name: Tails
distros.description: Amnesic live OS that routes traffic through Tor by default.

distros.name: Whonix
distros.description: Two-VM set up (Gateway + Workstation) tuned for Tor anonymity.

distros.name: Qubes OS
distros.description: Security-by-compartmentalization OS, running each app in its own Xen VM.

distros.name: Qubes Whonix
distros.description: Qubes OS template pairing with Whonix for enforced compartmentalized Tor use.

distros.name: Devuan
distros.description: Fork of Debian without systemd, using SysV init or OpenRC instead.

distros.name: Devuan Ceres
distros.description: Devuan's unstable / rolling release, the equivalent of Debian Sid.

distros.name: Refracta
distros.description: Devuan-derived easy-to-install distribution with snapshot tools.

distros.name: Exe GNU
distros.description: Devuan-based distro following strictly Free Software Distribution Guidelines.

distros.name: Trisquel
distros.description: Fully free Ubuntu-derivative endorsed by the Free Software Foundation.

distros.name: Trisquel Mini
distros.description: Lightweight Trisquel for older hardware.

distros.name: PureOS
distros.description: FSDG-compliant privacy-focused distro from Purism, often bundled with the Librem 5 phone.

distros.name: PureOS Mobile
distros.description: PureOS build target for the Librem 5 phone.

distros.name: Arch Linux
distros.description: Rolling-release DIY distro following the KISS principle. Upstream for many derivatives.

distros.name: Arch Linux ARM
distros.description: ARM port of Arch Linux for embedded and developer boards.

distros.name: Manjaro
distros.description: User-friendly Arch-based distro with curated repos and pre-installed desktops.

distros.description: Manjaro edition with the XFCE desktop.

distros.name: Manjaro KDE
distros.description: Manjaro edition with the KDE Plasma desktop.

distros.name: Manjaro GNOME
distros.description: Manjaro edition with the GNOME desktop.

distros.name: EndeavourOS
distros.description: Friendly Arch-based distro continuing the spirit of Antergos.

distros.name: EndeavourOS Cassini
distros.description: Official community edition.

distros.name: ArcoLinux
distros.description: Educational Arch derivative with extensive choice of DEs and community scripts.

distros.name: ArcoLinuxB
distros.description: Bare ArcoLinux installation ready for users to build their own customised OS.

distros.name: ArcoLinuxD
distros.description: ArcoLinux edition with KDE Plasma.

distros.name: Garuda Linux
distros.description: Performance- and gaming-leaning Arch derivative with striking visuals.

distros.name: Garuda Dragonized
distros.description: Garuda's hero edition with KDE Plasma.

distros.name: Garuda Gaming
distros.description: Gaming-optimized Garuda edition.

distros.name: Artix Linux
distros.description: Arch-derivative shipping OpenRC, Runit, and s6 init systems in place of systemd.

distros.description: Artix edition with runit init.

distros.name: Artix s6
distros.description: Artix edition with s6 init suite.

distros.name: BlackArch
distros.description: Arch-based security and pentesting distribution with 2800+ tools.

distros.name: CachyOS
distros.description: Performance-tuned Arch-derivative with custom BORE-cfs kernel.

distros.name: KDE neon KaOS
distros.description: Arch-based distro focused exclusively on one desktop (KDE). Originally called KaOS.

distros.name: Antergos

distros.name: Parabola GNU/Linux-libre

distros.name: Chakra

distros.name: RebornOS
distros.description: Friendly Arch-derivative continuing the Antergos spirit.

distros.name: Red Hat Enterprise Linux
distros.description: Commercial enterprise-grade Linux distribution from Red Hat.

distros.name: Fedora Linux
distros.description: Community-led upstream to RHEL, fast-moving with the latest GNU/Linux.

distros.name: Fedora Server

distros.name: Fedora Kinoite

distros.name: Fedora CoreOS
distros.description: Container-focused, automatically updating Fedora derivative.

distros.name: Nobara
distros.description: Tomasz Gloger's gaming- and productivity-tuned Fedora remix.

distros.name: Ultramarine Linux
distros.description: Community Fedora remix shipping GNOME, KDE, and Budgie.

distros.name: Korora
distros.description: Fedora remix tuned for the everyday user. Discontinued.

distros.name: Linpus Linux
distros.description: Asian-market, OEM-targeted distributions.

distros.name: BlueOnyx
distros.description: Server management distro with a web UI, derived from CentOS/Fedora. Discontinued.

distros.name: CentOS Linux
distros.description: Community rebuild of RHEL. CentOS Linux was discontinued in 2021 in favour of CentOS Stream.

distros.name: Rocky Linux
distros.description: RHEL-compatible community rebuild created by one of the original CentOS founders.

distros.name: AlmaLinux OS

distros.name: Oracle Linux
distros.description: RHEL-compatible, with optional Unbreakable Enterprise Kernel.

distros.name: Amazon Linux 2

distros.name: Clear Linux
distros.description: Intel's performance-tuned rolling distro for Intel architectures. Discontinued.

distros.name: Mandriva Linux
distros.description: Originally MandrakeSoft, French-Merger of Conectiva and Mandrake, pioneering the easy desktop Linux. Discontinued.

distros.description: Red Hat derivative aimed at newcomers. Renamed to Mandriva after merging with Conectiva.

distros.name: Porteus

distros.description: Lightweight, beginner-friendly Slackware-based distro. Discontinued.

distros.description: SUSE's enterprise-grade Linux server distro.

distros.description: Community-driven SUSE Linux.

distros.description: Beginner-friendly Gentoo-based binary distribution. Discontinued.

distros.description: Gentoo-based security and penetration-testing distribution.
